info.name: Pharmacy APIs
info.description:
Clean pharmacy module Postman collection aligned with the current backend routes.

Suggested publish flow:
1. Import this collection into Postman.
2. Add environment variables like `baseUrl`, `authToken`, `adminToken`, `pharmacyId`, `userId`, and medicine IDs.
3. Open collection documentation or publish with Postman Documenter.

Role notes:
- `adminToken`: admin-only operations.
- `authToken`: pharmacy or user token depending on the request you are testing.
- Some GET APIs return different fields based on the token role, especially pharmacy inventory and medicine availability APIs.

item.name: Pharmacy Signup Or Login - Send OTP

request.description: If the pharmacy does not exist yet, backend creates an unverified pharmacy profile and sends OTP. If it already exists, this works as pharmacy login OTP.

item.name: Pharmacy Signup Or Login - Verify OTP

request.description: Verifies the pharmacy OTP, returns auth token, and stores token/pharmacy IDs into collection variables.

Main pharmacy listing API. Use query parameters instead of separate verified/nearby endpoints wherever possible. For user-facing screens, pass filters like city, search, and verified status. Admin can use broader filters.

Shows pharmacies near the supplied coordinates that currently have the requested medicine in stock.

Role-based pricing:
- user: retail price only
- pharmacy/admin: retail and wholesale price

Use latitude/longitude/radius for accurate local search.
Get pharmacy details by ID. Access is restricted by backend rules. Use this when opening a pharmacy profile screen from list/search results.
Pharmacy self-profile. Requires a pharmacy token.

request.description: Pharmacy self-profile update. Use this after OTP signup to complete the profile and upload operational details/documents.
Browse the medicine master catalog. This is not pharmacy stock; it is the shared medicine list used before inventory is attached to a pharmacy.

Lists medicines available around the supplied coordinates, grouped at medicine level. Helpful for the user-facing `Medicines near me` tab.

Role-based pricing:
- user: returns retail-facing pricing summary
- pharmacy/admin: can also receive wholesale-facing summary

Use this first, then open pharmacy availability for a selected medicine.
Get a single medicine from the shared medicine catalog.

}

request.description:
Single inventory upsert API for pharmacy stock. Pass one or many items in `items`.

Behavior:
- creates inventory row if missing
- updates metadata if row exists
- applies quantity movement using positive or negative `quantity`
- no separate PUT needed for normal stock changes

Role-based inventory listing.

Behavior by token:
- admin: can query any pharmacy using `pharmacyId`, gets full internal inventory details
- pharmacy: sees its own internal inventory, `pharmacyId` optional if token is linked to pharmacy
- user: must pass `pharmacyId`, sees sanitized inventory only

User-facing fields are limited to medicine basics, available quantity, retail price, batch number, expiry date, and active status.
Internal operational API for admin/pharmacy to find stock below reorder threshold.
Manual medicine-level reserve for pharmacy internal use only. This is separate from order lifecycle logic.
Release manual medicine-level reserve created by pharmacy internal operations.
Fetch one internal inventory row by its inventory item ID. Mainly for admin/pharmacy detail/debug screens.

item.name: Pharmacy Cart

request.description:
Returns the current cart for the authenticated user or pharmacy user. Cart pricing is role-aware:
- user sees retail pricing
- pharmacy sees wholesale pricing when available, otherwise retail

Adds or updates one medicine in cart.

Quantity rules:
- positive quantity adds/increases
- negative quantity reduces
- if resulting quantity becomes 0 or below, item is removed
- quantity cannot be 0

Cart rule:
- cart accepts items from only one pharmacy
- adding from another pharmacy returns a clear mismatch error asking to clear cart first
Example for reducing quantity using a negative value. If quantity drops to 0, backend removes the item automatically.
Removes one medicine from cart completely.
Clears the whole cart. Use this before adding medicines from another pharmacy.

item.description: Single-pharmacy cart for user or pharmacy ordering. Cart enforces one pharmacy at a time.

body.raw:
{
  "orderType": "online",
  "customerId": "{{userId}}",
  "items": [
    {
      "medicineId": "{{medicineId}}",
      "medicineName": "Paracetamol 650",
      "orderedQty": 2,
      "discount": 1,
      "recurring": true,
      "subscriptionIntervalDays": 30,
      "subscriptionFrequency": "Daily after dinner",
      "subscriptionNextRefillDate": "20260810",
      "subscriptionReminderChannel": "both"
    }
  ],
  "paymentMode": "online_payment",
  "deliveryMode": "delivery",
  "deliveryAddress": "221B Baker Street, Delhi",
  "isWalkIn": false,
  "prescriptionUrls": ["https://example.com/prescription.pdf"],
  "notes": "Urgent delivery",
  "linkedConsultationId": "{{consultationId}}",
  "linkedDiagnosticOrderId": "{{diagnosticBookingId}}"
}

request.description: Create a pharmacy order. For subscription via order, only recurring item fields are needed on the medicines that should create subscriptions. Backend calculates pricing and returns payment context for online payment flows.

Role-aware order listing.
- pharmacy sees its own relevant orders through token-based access rules
- user/customer can see only their own orders
- admin can inspect broader order sets
Cancels a pharmacy order. Backend handles inventory rollback and downstream order effects according to current lifecycle rules.
Marks COD/in-store style pharmacy orders as paid. Intended for admin/pharmacy operational payment confirmation, not end users.
